source_display: Chris Hanson
evidence: Chris revealed he's been building his edtech product for almost a year, leveraging AI a lot

source_display: AI as ADHD Brain Crutch
evidence: I've always been so interested in building out stuff that essentially tries to act as a crutch. Like essentially the equivalent of like a brain crutch for someone with ADHD

source_display: Gap Between Therapy/Meds/Coaching and Daily Support
evidence: if traditionally the options available to us were therapy and medication, both great... Surely another avenue could be something that fills in the gaps or picks up behind you

source_display: Novelty vs Consistency Tightrope (ADHD)
evidence: I find myself trying to always toe this line between novelty and consistency, because I know that if I go too far in any direction... everything is novel every day is brand new. Holy shit. This is way too much

source_display: B2B-First EdTech Monetization (Orgs Pay, Users Free)
evidence: I have a moral obligation to not put more on supporters, on teachers... if they can use this for free and their employer will pay for it

source_display: No Custom Feature Builds for Enterprise
evidence: Decision: Decline custom feature builds for organizational clients. Context: Avoid over-optimizing for one stakeholder, manage bandwidth risk

source_display: Vrijen Attawar
evidence: I'm looking to build out more of an advising business... Let's have two or three conversations where we're at least just sounding each other out

source_display: Trust Navigation Challenges for Non-Technical Founders
evidence: The first year sucked ass because it was a lot of planting on my face and a lot of trusting people I shouldn't have trusted

source_display: Joe Coleman
evidence: Joe Coleman, he was a person that's sort of like our tech handyman and just helped us in a lot of different ways

source_display: Articulating Vision to Engineers as Core Founder Skill
evidence: You had to articulate that and that is such a difficult part of the process is saying I want this in ways that Joe will understand

source_display: Careerspan as 'Human Data Company'
evidence: V: So the demo is like human data. Like it's a human data oriented demo.

source_display: First-Party Opted-In Data as Strategic Moat
evidence: Boom, mic drop for the hiring people. This is opted in candidate data... as things heat up with publicly scraped data, this is a really valuable strategy for you guys

source_display: Show Don't Tell Strategy for Collateral
evidence: Can we make like a 30 second look at the information that we capture via Careerspan? Because if that's in an email... they don't have to wrap their heads around what it is we're describing. It's just like 'oh shit, I want that information.'

source_display: Video Demo Over Pitch Deck
evidence: I'm thinking a deck is not as impactful as watching it in action... Yeah, I don't think we should submit the deck.

source_display: Sourcing and Matching Collapse Into Same Thing
evidence: Sourcing and matching become the same thing

source_display: Internal Talent Development as New Competitive Edge
evidence: Internal talent development and talent identification is the new edge

source_display: Donna/Donatella: Story vs No-Story Demo Contrast
evidence: Donna and Donatella are the same person. One is with a story, one is without

source_display: Text Explanations Feel Like a Rope (Manipulation)
evidence: The intrinsic problem with even trying to generate it that way or trying to put anything in text is the moment we try to explain, it just has to come off as like a rope on some level

source_display: Baller Move: Give Value Upfront in Outreach
evidence: It's like kind of a baller move to put the lead list of workforce boards in. That's kind of like just giving value up front

source_display: Careerspan as Synthetic Talent Pipeline You Can Scan
evidence: With Careerspan, you're building a synthetic talent pipeline that you can scan at any moment

source_display: Careerspan
evidence: Fuck third party work. First party.

source_display: The Best Hire is Who You Already Have
evidence: The best hire is who you already have

source_display: Two-Segment Collateral Strategy (Sourcing vs Internal Mobility)
evidence: Two distinct video/message versions: 1. Sourcing/Matching/External Hiring — for recruiting teams 2. Internal Mobility — for enterprise HR/L&D
